core_meaning: Passive voice with 'par' emphasizing action recipient with explicit agent.
primary_communicative_fn: To emphasize the receiver of an action rather than the performer
when_to_use: Use when the agent is unknown, unimportant, or when the recipient is the focus; common in scientific, administrative, and journalistic texts
pattern: Sujet + être + participe passé + par + agent (optionnel)
key_variations: Present: La loi est modifiée par le Parlement | Past: Le pont a été construit par les romains | Future: Le projet sera présenté par le directeur | Conditional: Le rapport serait accepté par tous
essential_vocabulary_slots: agent (performer): le gouvernement, les scientifiques, l'entreprise | recipient: la loi, la décision, le rapport | common verbs: modifier, accepter, voter, construire, écrire
common_learner_mistakes: Forgetting the agreement of past participle with subject when used with avoir in compound tenses; confusing agent markers (par vs de); using passive in spoken French where it's too formal
chunk_compatibility: Passive patterns: Est modifiée par, A été construit par, Sera présenté par, Serait accepté par
key_forms: est modifiée par, a été construit par, sera présenté par, serait accepté par
essential_vocabulary: être, par, modifié, construit, présenté, accepté, gouvernement, scientifiques
why_it_matters: Essential for formal written French; emphasizes action recipient when agent is unknown or unimportant.
common_mistakes: Using active when passive needed; confusing 'par' with 'de'; not agreeing past participle.

core_meaning: Passive voice with 'de' for emotional or descriptive qualities of the agent.
primary_communicative_fn: To indicate agent characterized by quality or emotional relationship
when_to_use: Use 'de' when the agent is described by an adjective or is an emotional/personal descriptor rather than a true agent performing action
pattern: Sujet + être + participe passé + de + agent descriptif
key_variations: Estimé de tous | Respecté de ses pairs | Aimé de sa communauté | Apprécié de ses collègues
essential_vocabulary_slots: adjectives: estimé, respecté, aimé, craint, détesté, honoré | with nouns: suivi de près, assisté de, accompagné de
common_learner_mistakes: Confusing par and de; using de with true agents (actions, not emotions); overgeneralizing de usage
chunk_compatibility: Emotional patterns: Estimé de tous, Respecté de ses pairs, Aimé de sa communauté, Craint de l'ennemi
key_forms: estimé de, respecté de, aimé de, craint de, apprécié de
essential_vocabulary: estimé, respecté, aimé, craint, détesté, honoré, pairs, communauté, collègues
why_it_matters: Distinguishes emotional/quality-based agents from true action agents; essential for accurate passive voice.
common_mistakes: Confusing 'par' and 'de'; using 'de' for action agents; not recognizing emotional quality.

core_meaning: Pronominal passive with 'se' expressing passive meaning with indefinite subject.
primary_communicative_fn: To express passive meaning with indefinite or generic subject
when_to_use: Use when the subject is indefinite (on, quelqu'un) and the action affects the subject directly; common in official and scientific language
pattern: On + se + verbe (à la voix passive) | Sujet indéterminé + se + verbe
key_variations: Ces livres se vendent bien (=on vend ces livres) | Cette politique se révise régulièrement (=on révise cette politique) | Le français s'apprend facilement (=on apprend le français)
essential_vocabulary_slots: verbs that allow this: vendre, acheter, servir, réviser, traduire, comprendre | typically transitive verbs with thing/object subjects
common_learner_mistakes: Confusing with reflexive pronoun for reciprocal meaning; not recognizing when passive pronominal is possible; overusing this form
chunk_compatibility: Passive patterns: Se vendent bien, Se révise régulièrement, S'apprend facilement, Se fait en...
key_forms: se vendent, se révise, s'apprend, se fait, se traduit
essential_vocabulary: se, vendre, acheter, servir, réviser, traduire, comprendre, apprendre
why_it_matters: Pronominal passive is common in scientific and formal French; more elegant than 'on' construction.
common_mistakes: Confusing with reflexive verbs; not recognizing it as passive; misusing with intransitive verbs.

core_meaning: Converting verbs to nouns to create abstract nominal expressions for formal style.
primary_communicative_fn: To transform action-based sentences into noun-based expressions for more abstract, formal style
when_to_use: Use in formal writing, academic contexts, administrative language; nominalizations create denser, more abstract text
pattern: Verbe → Nom: décider → la décision | choisir → le choix | accepter → l'acceptation | modifier → la modification
key_variations: choisir → le choix | accepter → l'acceptation | modifier → la modification | développer → le développement
essential_vocabulary_slots: common conversions: faire → la fabrication | créer → la création | mourir → le décès | naître → la naissance
common_learner_mistakes: Overusing nominalizations making text heavy; incorrect noun form selection; not knowing the noun form of irregular verbs
chunk_compatibility: Nominalization patterns: La décision de..., La création de..., Le développement de..., L'acceptation de...
key_forms: la décision, le choix, l'acceptation, la modification, la création, la naissance
essential_vocabulary: décider, choisir, accepter, modifier, développer, créer, mourir, naître
why_it_matters: Nominalization is essential for formal academic and administrative French; creates more abstract, concise text.
common_mistakes: Overusing nominalizations; incorrect noun forms; making text too dense and hard to follow.

core_meaning: Converting adjectives to nouns expressing qualities as abstract concepts.
primary_communicative_fn: To express qualities as nouns for more formal, concise expression
when_to_use: Use in formal contexts when expressing a quality as a concept rather than describing something; creates abstract, academic style
pattern: Adjectif → Nom dérivé: ancien → l'ancien | heureux → le bonheur | capable → la capacité
key_variations: intelligent → l'intelligence | difficile → la difficulté | indépendant → l'indépendance | généreux → la générosité
essential_vocabulary_slots: adjective to noun patterns: -ant/-ent → -ance/-ence (constant → constance) | -eux/-euse → -eur (joyeux → joie) | -able/-ible → -abilité (flexible → flexibilité)
common_learner_mistakes: Not knowing the noun form of adjectives; confusing masculine/feminine noun forms; using adjective where noun is needed
chunk_compatibility: Nominalization patterns: L'intelligence, La capacité, L'indépendance, La générosité, La difficulté
key_forms: l'intelligence, la capacité, l'indépendance, la générosité, la difficulté
essential_vocabulary: intelligent, capable, indépendant, généreux, difficile, ancien, heureux, intelligent
why_it_matters: Essential for transforming adjectives into abstract nouns; common in academic and formal writing.
common_mistakes: Not knowing adjective-to-noun forms; confusing gender; using adjective instead of noun.

core_meaning: Using infinitive as noun expressing action while maintaining verbal flexibility.
primary_communicative_fn: To nominalize actions expressed as verbs while keeping verbal flexibility
when_to_use: Use when you want verbal action expressed as noun concept but still need to keep the action dynamic rather than static
pattern: le + infinitif = nom: le manger, le dormir, le travailler
key_variations: Le comprendre est essentiel | Le savoir-faire compte | Le non-faire | L'être plutôt que le paraître
essential_vocabulary_slots: common uses: le manger, le dormir (quantifying habits) | le savoir-faire (competence) | l'avoir (possessions) | l'essence de l'être
common_learner_mistakes: Overusing infinitive nominalization; using article incorrectly (le vs un); confusing with English '-ing' forms
chunk_compatibility: Nominalized infinitive patterns: Le manger, Le dormir, Le savoir-faire, Le comprendre, L'être
key_forms: le manger, le dormir, le savoir-faire, le comprendre, l'être
essential_vocabulary: le, manger, dormir, savoir-faire, comprendre, travailler, être, avoir
why_it_matters: Allows verbs to function as nouns; useful in philosophical and abstract contexts; creates formal style.
common_mistakes: Overusing infinitive nominalization; incorrect article usage; confusing with gerund.

primary_communicative_fn: Creating nouns from other parts of speech to express concepts
when_to_use: Use when needing to express abstract concepts; common in academic and administrative French
pattern: Root + suffixe → nom: aim-er → aim-ant → aim-ation | libre → libr-ité | grand → grand-eur
key_variations: -tion/-sion: organiser → organisation | discuter → discussion | comprendre → compréhension | -ment: juger → jugement | payer → paiement | -eur: conduire → conducteur | protéger → protecteur
essential_vocabulary_slots: common suffixes: -tion, -sion, -ment, -ure, -ité, -eur, -ance, -ence, -esse | irregular: heureux → bonheur, ancien → ancienneté
common_learner_mistakes: Not knowing correct suffix for each root; mixing up similar suffixes; irregular formations

primary_communicative_fn: Creating adjectives from nouns and verbs to describe qualities
when_to_use: Use when needing to describe a quality derived from a noun or specify a noun's characteristics
pattern: Nom + suffixe → adj: danger → danger-eux | pore → por-eux | culture → cultur-el
key_variations: -eux/-euse: amour → amour-eux | pore → poreux | danger → dangereux | -el/-elle: nation → national | monde → mondial | -able/-ible: accept → acceptable |irascible | -ant/-ent: importance → important | différence → différent
essential_vocabulary_slots: common suffixes: -eux, -el, -al, -able, -ible, -ant, -ent, -aire, -aire | examples: histoire → historique | industrie → industriel | nation → national
common_learner_mistakes: Not knowing which suffix applies; confusing similar suffixes; irregular formations

primary_communicative_fn: Creating adverbs to modify verbs, adjectives, or other adverbs
when_to_use: Use when you need to indicate manner, time, place, or degree of an action or quality
pattern: Adjectif + -ment → adverbe: rapide → rapidement | heureux → malheureusement
key_variations: regular: précis → précisément | clair → clairement | facile → facilement | exception: bon → bien (irregular) | vrai → vraiment | bref → brièvement
essential_vocabulary_slots: feminine form + ment: généreux → généreusement | précis → précisément |特殊: -ant endings: patient → patiemment | présent → présentement (but souvent is exception)
common_learner_mistakes: Using masculine form + ment; irregular adverb forms; confusion with English adverbs ending in -ly

structure_label: 文章标题: nom composé (compound nouns)
core_meaning: Compound nouns formed from noun + noun combinations
primary_communicative_fn: Creating single concept from two nouns for precise naming
when_to_use: Use for technical terms, compound meanings, specific designations
pattern: Nom + nom → nom composé: chef-lieu | timbre-poste | coffre-fort
key_variations: avec trait d'union: chou-fleur | sans trait: salle de bain (but now normally with) | plural rules: des choux-fleurs (both parts agree)
essential_vocabulary_slots: common compounds: timbre-poste, chef-lieu, coffre-fort, arc-boutant, basse-cour, timbre-poste | meanings: wagon-lit (sleeping car) | chemin de fer (railway)
common_learner_mistakes: Not knowing when to use hyphen; incorrect plural formation; treating compound as two separate words

structure_label: 文章标题: nom composé (noun + préposition + nom)
core_meaning: Compound nouns with preposition in between
primary_communicative_fn: Creating compound nouns that express relationship requiring preposition
when_to_use: Use when the relationship between concepts requires preposition for clarity
pattern: Nom + préposition + nom:arc de triomphe | salle de classe | machine à laver
key_variations: à: machine à café | clé à molette |café à emporter | de: salle de bain |arc de cercle | pain de mie | en:arc-en-ciel | chemin de fer
essential_vocabulary_slots: common patterns: à (purpose/function) | de (material, content) | en (color) | special: chemin de fer, arc-en-ciel
common_learner_mistakes: Forgetting preposition; using wrong preposition; treating as separate words

structure_label: 文章标题: participe présent comme adjectif
core_meaning: Present participle used as adjective with variable agreement
primary_communicative_fn: Converting action to description while maintaining verbal relationship
when_to_use: Use when describing a quality resulting from ongoing action; present participle as adjective agrees with noun
pattern: Nom + -ant/-ente (accordé): des enfants dorm-ant → des enfants dormant | une histoire int-éress-ante → intéressante
key_variations: accord complet: une femme charmante | hommes charmants | sans accord (après préposition): en attendant | tout en dormant | sens actif: un bruit agaçant (=qui agace)
essential_vocabulary_slots: common examples: un bruit agaçant | une histoire intéressante | des résultats encourageants | en attendant (=while waiting)
common_learner_mistakes: Not agreeing when used as adjective; confusing with infinitive; overusing as adjective instead of proper clause

structure_label: 文章标题: participe passé comme adjectif
core_meaning: Past participle used as adjective with fixed agreement
primary_communicative_fn: Converting completed action to descriptive quality
when_to_use: Use when describing a state resulting from completed action; past participle as adjective agrees with noun
pattern: Nom + -é/-ée/-és/-ées (accordé): une lettre écr-it → une lettre écrite | des lettre écr-ites
key_variations: avec avoir: la lettre que j'ai écrite (accord avec objet direct precedant) | adjectif verbal: une chose faite | États-Unis est unstati | differences:印象的 vs inquiété (different verbs from same root)
essential_vocabulary_slots: common examples: une lettre cachetée | un travail accompli | une décision prise | des résultats obtenus | wound:印象的 (=impressive) vs inquiet (=worried)
common_learner_mistakes: Agreement errors with avoir; not distinguishing past participle from adjective; confusing adjective with verbal participle

structure_label: 文章标题: 名词化 (nominalization) des propositions
core_meaning: Turning clauses into noun phrases for nominal style
primary_communicative_fn: Converting sentences to noun phrases for formal, condensed expression
when_to_use: Use in formal academic and administrative writing when you need to compress information or make text more abstract
pattern: Clause → that + nom: Je suis venu → le fait que je sois venu | Il a réussi → sa réussite
key_variations: infinitive: Sortir tôt est important | Que + subj: Qu'il vienne est essentiel | Détinir: le fait de sortir | nom correspondant: Il a promis → sa promesse
essential_vocabulary_slots: facts: le fait que (+subjunctive) | le fait de (+infinitive) | actions: arriver → l'arrivée | partir → le départ | promettre → la promesse |的评价: intéressant → l'intérêt de
common_learner_mistakes: Wrong mood after 'le fait que' (should be subjunctive); improper infinitive use; awkward nominalization of certain verbs
